nativeRange.habitat: Shallow streams, pond margins, and seasonally flooded wetlands across tropical Central and South America; grows submerged, emersed, or floating on still and slow-moving water

decorPreferences: open midground or background space for trailing stems
open surface area if grown floating

goodWith: fry that benefit from floating cover
avoidWith: plant-eating fish such as goldfish, large cichlids, or silver dollars

summary: Hydrocotyle leucocephala (Brazilian Pennywort) is a versatile beginner-friendly stem plant with round, coin-shaped leaves on creeping stems. It can be planted in substrate, left to float, or trained to climb hardscape, and grows fast under medium light.
careNotes: Hydrocotyle leucocephala is a fast-growing stem plant native to Central and South America, recognizable by its round, coin-shaped leaves arranged singly along a long creeping stem. It is one of the most versatile aquarium plants: it can be planted in substrate as a midground or background accent, left to float at the surface where it forms shade and cover for fry, or trained to trail along driftwood and rockwork. This is distinct from the related Hydrocotyle verticillata (fw-plant-031), which has round leaves arranged in whorls and grows as a strict low creeper, and from Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides (fw-plant-071), which is a much smaller carpeting species. Care is generally beginner-level: it tolerates a wide range of conditions (temperature roughly 20-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, soft to moderately hard water) and does not require CO2, though added CO2, strong light, and a nutrient-rich substrate produce denser, more compact growth. Iron supplementation is commonly advised if leaves yellow at the edges. Cross-source notes: family was given as 'Apiaceae' by Buce Plant but 'Araliaceae' by Flowgrow — modern APG taxonomy places Hydrocotyle in Araliaceae, which is used here. Buce Plant also listed the origin as 'Asia', which is incorrect; Flowgrow and Modern Aquarium both confirm Central and South American origin, and the species epithet and common name (Brazilian Pennywort) make Brazil the type locality. Lighting was rated 'medium' (Buce Plant, Modern Aquarium) and 'medium to high' (Flowgrow); 'medium' was adopted as the cross-source consensus since all sources agree the plant tolerates lower light and only grows denser (not requires) under stronger light. CO2 was 'required' per Buce Plant but 'not required, beneficial' per Modern Aquarium and Flowgrow — 'optional' was chosen as the consensus. Care level was rated 'easy to moderate' (Buce Plant), 'easy' (Modern Aquarium), and 'very easy, suitable for beginners' (Flowgrow) — 'beginner' was adopted. Adult size 13-25 cm reflects Modern Aquarium's 5-10 inch description; the plant can grow much longer trailing stems if left untrimmed. Minimum tank volume of 40 L is a conservative floor for a small planted setup and is not directly stated by any source.
breedingNotes: Propagation is straightforward via vegetative cuttings: cut the creeping stem into segments and replant, or separate lateral shoots/daughter plants from a healthy mother plant. The species throws many lateral shoots along the stem that can be trimmed off anywhere and replanted, and frequent trimming encourages bushier, more compact growth.

primary.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
primary.notes: Used for taxonomy (Araliaceae / Apiales), temperature (5-30 °C, optimum 20-28 °C), pH (6-7.5), kH (2-15), CO2 (10-40 mg/l), lighting (medium to high), growth rate (fast), care difficulty (very easy), and propagation.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked pH (6.5-7.5), lighting (medium), CO2 (required), growth rate (moderate to fast), placement, propagation, and adult size (5-10 inches). Lists family as 'Apiaceae' (older taxonomy) and origin as 'Asia' — both rejected in favor of Flowgrow's modern taxonomy and the species' well-known Neotropical origin.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (59-82 °F = ~15-28 °C), lighting (medium to high), CO2 (not required, beneficial), growth rate (fast), placement versatility (midground, foreground, floating, trailing on hardscape), care level (easy), and leaf size (1-2 inch coin-shaped leaves).
